---
title: "timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-23
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [8, 5, 5, 17, 14, 14, 37]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![2x3 grid of day-10-vs-day-5 codon Fisher volcano panels: rows are conditions (top = BWM, bottom = control); columns are ribosome sites (A / P / E). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) against log2(odds ratio); triangles mark FDR<0.05 hits and circles are non-significant. Glyphs are coloured by codon biochemistry (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple). The control,P panel carries the file's tallest single point; control,A is the next most-populated panel. The BWM row is sparse; **BWM,E is completely null** — every glyph is a circle below the FDR=0.05 line. The control row is densely populated across all three sites.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/codon/composite/codon_fisher_composite.png)

Each glyph is a single codon at one (condition, site) BH-FDR family of 61; horizontal dashed line is FDR = 0.05 and vertical dashed lines mark +/-0.5 log2(OR). Positive log2(OR) means day-10-enriched relative to day-5 *within the same condition*; negative means day-10-depleted relative to day-5. The test does not compare BWM to control directly — that contrast is implied by reading paired top/bottom rows of the grid side-by-side. Panels are arranged in A / P / E ribosome-site order across columns.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_aa.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_codon.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_aa.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), day-10-vs-day-5 2x2 contingency per (condition, site, codon); BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 61 codons
- **Design:** Per (condition, site, codon) 2x2 day-10-vs-day-5 contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact within one condition; replicates summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 6 (condition, site) families of 61 codons (366 tests total). The test is **within-condition**: BWM and control are tested separately and never share a contingency.

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 biological reps per (condition, timepoint) are summed into the 2x2 before Fisher's exact; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — per-timepoint per-condition totals are in the thousands (BWM: day_10=6945, day_5=11935; control: day_10=8788, day_5=11177); Fisher returns vanishing p-values for tiny absolute deviations, so `log2_OR` is the primary effect column, not p magnitude.
- **bh-per-(condition,site)** — BH-FDR is computed independently within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of 61 codons; `p_adj` means "corrected within this sub-family", not across the full 366-row file.
- **within-condition-clean** — unlike the between-condition or between-timepoint Wilcoxon families, no condition pooling and no timepoint pooling cross-contaminate the test; the design is structurally cleaner.

---

## Headline

d10 vs d5 within-condition Fisher (codon-level): 22/366 hits at FDR<0.05 (4/183 BWM, 18/183 control). BWM concentrates on site-A and site-P GGA enrichment (A:GGA `log2_OR`=+0.382 FDR<5e-4, P:GGA `log2_OR`=+0.356 FDR<2.4e-3) plus two rare-codon depletions (A:CTG `log2_OR`=-1.419 FDR=0.028; P:TTT `log2_OR`=-1.023 FDR=0.035). Control sweeps broadly across all three sites (A: 5 hits dominated by GGA enriched / CAA depleted / AAC depleted plus GCC and CCA enriched; E: 7 hits including GCC/CTC/CCA/ACC/GTC/ATC enriched and GAG depleted; P: 6 hits with GGA `log2_OR`=+0.516 FDR<6e-9 the leading feature plus CAA/ATT/GTC/CTT/ATC depleted). The site-A and site-P GGA enrichments are the only shared-direction-significant cells in this CSV: same direction in BWM and control across both sites and at large magnitude. Notable direction flips with one arm significant and the other arm trending the opposite way but non-significant: P:GTC (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.330 ns vs control `log2_OR`=-0.426 FDR=0.017) carries the larger BWM-side magnitude; E:GAG (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.107 ns vs control `log2_OR`=-0.292 FDR=0.0069) trends opposite only marginally on the BWM side.

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_OR` (log2 of the Fisher odds ratio; positive = day-10-enriched, negative = day-10-depleted within one condition). `p_value` is the raw Fisher's exact two-sided p; `p_adj` is BH-corrected within each (condition, site) family of 61 codons. Direction is fixed by the day-10-vs-day-5 contingency layout within one condition: `log2_OR` > 0 = day-10-enriched relative to day-5 in that condition, `log2_OR` < 0 = day-10-depleted. Sub-tables below are split per-direction within each condition (4 sub-tables total, BWM/control x Enriched/Depleted). The `site` column carries the ribosome site; rows are grouped by site in canonical A / P / E order, then sorted by `p_adj` ascending with `|log2_OR|` descending as the tie-breaker. All rows with `p_adj` < 0.05 are shown; no row cap and no raw-p fallback (family-level rule for `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`). The `aa` column is the single-letter amino acid translation of each codon.

```{=latex}
\needspace{10\baselineskip}
```

### BWM — Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| codon | aa | site | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| GGA | G | A | 0.382 | 7.68e-06 | 4.68e-04 |  |
| GGA | G | P | 0.356 | 3.92e-05 | 0.00239 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{10\baselineskip}
```

### BWM — Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| codon | aa | site | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CTG | L | A | -1.419 | 9.05e-04 | 0.0276 | rare-codon-low-count |
| TTT | F | P | -1.023 | 1.14e-03 | 0.0347 | rare-codon-low-count |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### control — Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| codon | aa | site | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| GGA | G | A | 0.435 | 4.32e-08 | 1.32e-06 |  |
| GCC | A | A | 0.474 | 1.00e-03 | 0.0154 |  |
| CCA | P | A | 0.338 | 2.60e-03 | 0.0320 |  |
| GGA | G | P | 0.516 | 9.35e-11 | 5.71e-09 |  |
| GCC | A | E | 0.613 | 5.02e-05 | 0.00111 |  |
| CTC | L | E | 0.508 | 4.96e-05 | 0.00111 |  |
| CCA | P | E | 0.472 | 5.44e-05 | 0.00111 |  |
| ACC | T | E | 0.479 | 5.54e-04 | 0.00691 |  |
| GTC | V | E | 0.323 | 3.30e-03 | 0.0338 |  |
| ATC | I | E | 0.293 | 4.60e-03 | 0.0401 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### control — Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| codon | aa | site | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CAA | Q | A | -0.857 | 1.67e-08 | 1.02e-06 |  |
| AAC | N | A | -0.568 | 1.48e-05 | 3.00e-04 |  |
| CAA | Q | P | -0.568 | 3.88e-04 | 0.0118 |  |
| ATT | I | P | -0.545 | 6.31e-04 | 0.0128 |  |
| GTC | V | P | -0.426 | 1.12e-03 | 0.0170 |  |
| CTT | L | P | -0.395 | 3.80e-03 | 0.0390 |  |
| ATC | I | P | -0.304 | 3.80e-03 | 0.0390 |  |
| GAG | E | E | -0.292 | 5.66e-04 | 0.00691 |  |

### Flag glossary

- `rare-codon-low-count` — at least one timepoint cell for this (condition, site, codon) row has `day_X_count` below the test's stability threshold (typically < 50 at codon resolution). `log2_OR` estimates and CIs are unstable at small k; magnitude weight should account for the count rather than rank by `log2_OR` alone. Applied here to BWM,A,CTG (day_10=12, day_5=55) and BWM,P,TTT (day_10=25, day_5=87).

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 366 (183 per condition; BH families of 61 codons per (condition, site))
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 22 (BWM 4, control 18)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 29 (BWM 9, control 20)
- `min adjusted-p`: 5.71e-09 (control, P, GGA)
- Per (condition, site) at FDR<0.05:
  - BWM,A: 2 (GGA enriched, CTG depleted); min p_adj = 4.68e-04
  - BWM,P: 2 (GGA enriched, TTT depleted); min p_adj = 0.00239
  - BWM,E: 0 (smallest p_adj = 0.114)
  - control,A: 5 (GGA, GCC, CCA enriched; CAA, AAC depleted); min p_adj = 1.02e-06 (CAA depleted)
  - control,P: 6 (GGA enriched; CAA, ATT, GTC, CTT, ATC depleted); min p_adj = 5.71e-09 (GGA enriched)
  - control,E: 7 (GCC, CTC, CCA, ACC, GTC, ATC enriched; GAG depleted); min p_adj = 0.00111 (3-way tie at GCC/CTC/CCA)

## Biological interpretation

**Site-A and site-P GGA enrichment is the file's largest shared-direction codon-level cell and is GGA-only, not pan-Gly (concrete).** BWM and control both significantly enrich GGA at sites A and P from day-5 to day-10 (BWM,A,GGA `log2_OR`=+0.382 `p_adj`=4.7e-4; BWM,P,GGA `log2_OR`=+0.356 `p_adj`=2.4e-3; control,A,GGA `log2_OR`=+0.435 `p_adj`=1.3e-6; control,P,GGA `log2_OR`=+0.516 `p_adj`=5.7e-9 — the file minimum). No other Gly synonym (GGC, GGG, GGT) clears FDR at either site in either condition — the aa-level d10-vs-d5 Gly enrichment at sites A and P is therefore carried entirely by GGA. GGA is a Gly synonym whose decoding involves a wobble interaction at the third position; a coordinated GGA-specific accumulation at the A and P sites between d5 and d10 in *both* conditions is most parsimonious with a shared time-axis shift in Gly-tRNA isoacceptor availability or in transcriptome composition (more GGA-encoding transcripts in the ribosome footprint pool late in the timecourse), not with a perturbation-specific effect.

**BWM site E is fully null at codon resolution (concrete; design-level observation).** All 61 BWM,E codons return `p_adj` >= 0.114, while the aa-level companion shows BWM,E:G, BWM,E:R, BWM,E:I significant at FDR<0.05 over the same contrast. This is the textbook `larger-bh-family` caveat at work: codon resolution multiplies the BH family from 20 (aa) to 61 (codons), and BWM's d10-vs-d5 site-E aa-level signal spreads across multiple Gly/Arg/Ile synonyms thinly enough that no single codon clears the per-(condition, site) FDR threshold. Biologically this rules out a clean synonym-preference signature at BWM,E for this contrast (otherwise one or two codons would have concentrated the aa-level signal and survived the larger BH family); the BWM,E aa-level effect, to the extent it is real, is *isoacceptor-spread* rather than codon-selective. Worth reading codon-vs-aa `p_adj` here on an effect-size axis, not on a p-axis.

**Control's broad d10-vs-d5 codon reshuffle concentrates at site E and site A (concrete; partly amplified by large-N).** Control fires 7 codon hits at site E (GCC/CTC/CCA enriched at FDR<1.2e-3 each; ACC, GTC, ATC also enriched; GAG depleted), 6 at site P (GGA enriched as the leader, plus CAA/ATT/GTC/CTT/ATC depleted), and 5 at site A (CAA the leading depletion at FDR<1.1e-6, plus AAC depleted, GGA/GCC/CCA enriched). The dominant statistical concern is the `large-N-Fisher-anticonservative` flag — control's pooled totals are ~12,000 per cell at this contrast, so the smallest p magnitudes (control,P,GGA at 5.7e-9, control,A,CAA at 1.0e-6) reflect tiny absolute deviations against a very large denominator, not necessarily large biological effect. Reading on the `log2_OR` axis, control hits span +0.29 to +0.61 (enriched) and -0.29 to -0.86 (depleted), none extreme. The control-only-significant cells where BWM trends in the same direction but stays ns (E:CTC, E:CCA, P:CAA, P:ATT, A:CAA, A:AAC per the `control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction` caveat block) suggest the control reshuffle is partly a shared time-axis trend that BWM is power-limited to detect at codon resolution.

**P:GTC is the primary codon-level direction-flip in this contrast; E:GAG trends opposite only marginally (exploratory).** Two cells have BWM trending opposite to control with one arm significant: P:GTC (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.330 ns p_value=0.0104 / p_adj=0.159 vs control `log2_OR`=-0.426 FDR=0.017), E:GAG (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.107 ns vs control `log2_OR`=-0.292 FDR=0.0069). Both have BWM on the opposite side of `log2_OR`=0 but non-significant, so calling them "flips" leans on control's significance carrying the contrast. E:GAG's BWM-side magnitude (+0.107) is close enough to zero that the opposite-direction reading is essentially noise; P:GTC carries the larger BWM-side magnitude (+0.330) and is the substantive flip candidate. GTC is a Val synonym, and a BWM-vs-control split at site P on a single Val codon does not have an obvious mechanistic anchor in the standard ribosome-pausing repertoire (no eIF5A/EF-P anchor for Val; no exit-tunnel electrostatic anchor; no obvious rare-codon decoding bottleneck given GTC is not rare in the *C. elegans* codon table). Treat as a codon-level pattern worth checking in the d10_vs_d0_codon and d5_vs_d0_codon sister CSVs — if P:GTC carries the same direction-flip across two contrasts in control while BWM stays ns, the cross-contrast reproducibility raises confidence; if not, the call is most consistent with control's broader site-P reshuffle landing on GTC by chance.

**BWM,P,TTT depletion (`rare-codon-low-count` flagged) is exploratory only.** BWM,P,TTT at `log2_OR`=-1.023, `p_adj`=0.035 has rare-codon counts (day_10=25 vs day_5=87). The cell crosses the FDR threshold but the `rare-codon-low-count` flag means the `log2_OR` magnitude is unstable; quote the direction (BWM depletes TTT at P between d5 and d10), do not quote the magnitude. There is no obvious mechanistic anchor for Phe-TTT specifically at site P in this contrast, and the cell does not appear paired with consistent FDR signal in any sister BWM column at the codon level.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the GGA-only A-site and P-site enrichment shared between BWM and control as a strong codon-usage signature in the d5->d10 window; control's site-E and site-A broad reshuffle (largest cells are control,P,GGA `log2_OR`=+0.516 file-min, control,A,CAA `log2_OR`=-0.857, control,E,GCC/CTC/CCA enrichments at `log2_OR`=+0.47 to +0.61); the BWM,E codon-level null as a `larger-bh-family` design observation rather than biology. *Exploratory*: P:GTC and E:GAG as candidate direction flips worth checking in the d10_vs_d0 and d5_vs_d0 codon sister contrasts (control significant in one direction; BWM trending opposite but ns). *Noise*: the `rare-codon-low-count`-flagged rows (BWM,A,CTG `log2_OR`=-1.419; BWM,P,TTT `log2_OR`=-1.023) — quote direction only, not magnitude.

## Methods
Fisher's exact (two-sided) is applied to a 2x2 of (codon_count, total - codon_count) at each timepoint within one condition. The script (`stall_sites_non_consensus_stats.py`) computes BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 61 codons at codon resolution (so each of the 6 sub-families is corrected independently, not against the full 366). **BWM site E is fully null at FDR<0.05** (smallest p_adj=0.114) — the d10_vs_d5 contrast has no detectable codon-level shift at site E in BWM, even though the aa-level d10_vs_d5 sister file shows E:G/E:R/E:I significant. This is consistent with codon-usage-shift features being washed out when aggregated to amino acids: small effects at multiple synonyms can sum to a sig aa-level effect even when no single synonym is sig. Counts (`day_X_count`) and totals (`day_X_total`) are summed across replicates before the test, hence the family-level pseudoreplication caveat.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** 2x2 contingencies pool replicates by design; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative — read `log2_OR` as the primary effect column and treat the smallest p-magnitudes as inflated, not as evidence of additional certainty.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** Per-timepoint per-condition totals are in the thousands (BWM: day_10=6945, day_5=11935; control: day_10=8788, day_5=11177); control,P,GGA hits `p_adj`=5.7e-9 at `log2_OR`=+0.516 (roughly a 43% relative shift). Rank by `log2_OR` when ordering effects; do not over-read p magnitude.
- **bh-per-(condition,site).** BH is run independently within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of 61 codons; two rows with the same raw p in different (condition, site) families can land at different `p_adj` — rank within a (condition, site) cell, not across the full 366-test grid.
- **within-condition-clean.** No condition pooling and no timepoint pooling; the file structurally answers "does day_10 differ from day_5 *within* this condition" cleanly, leaving the BWM-vs-control comparison to be done by reading paired panels side-by-side rather than by the test itself.
- **rare-codon-low-count.** Both BWM Top-depleted rows under the strict `p_adj` < 0.05 cutoff are rare-codon flagged (BWM,A,CTG day_10=12 / day_5=55; BWM,P,TTT day_10=25 / day_5=87). Rank by direction; flag magnitude as unstable for these rows.
- **OR-direction-asymmetry.** No zero-cell rows in this file (all day_X_count > 0), so no infinite/zero ORs; the caveat is preserved as a ranking-axis discipline note — `log2_OR` is the reported effect column (already log-transformed), so positive/negative bands are symmetric by construction; rank directly on it.
- **control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction.** Two direction flips with control significant and BWM trending opposite but ns: P:GTC (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.330 ns p_value=0.0104 / p_adj=0.159 vs control `log2_OR`=-0.426 FDR=0.017); E:GAG (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.107 ns vs control `log2_OR`=-0.292 FDR=0.0069). Several control-only-significant cells where BWM trends in the same direction but stays ns (E:CTC, E:CCA, P:CAA, P:ATT, A:CAA, A:AAC) — read these as control reshuffle that BWM is power-limited to detect.
- **larger-bh-family.** BH-FDR per (condition, site) family of 61 codons here vs 20 amino acids in the aa companion. Textbook case in this file: BWM site E is fully null at FDR<0.05 (smallest p_adj=0.114) while the aa companion shows BWM,E:G/R/I significant — the larger BH family wipes out per-synonym significance even when aa-level signal exists. A codon `p_adj`=0.114 here may carry the same evidence weight as an aa `p_adj` ~ 0.04 in the aa companion.

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-10-vs-day-5 BWM A-site codon volcano: log2(OR) on x-axis (range +/-12; data clusters within +/-2), -log10(FDR) on y; triangles are FDR<0.05. GGA (Gly, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~ +0.38, -log10(FDR) ~ 3.3 is the single largest enriched FDR<0.05 hit; CTG (Leu, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~ -1.4, -log10(FDR) ~ 1.6 is the only FDR<0.05 depleted hit (rare-codon flagged). All other codons collapse to a tight cluster near the origin below the FDR=0.05 line.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/codon/individual/BWM_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-5 BWM P-site codon volcano: GGA (Gly, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~ +0.35, -log10(FDR) ~ 2.7 is the largest enriched FDR<0.05 hit; TTT (Phe, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~ -1.0, -log10(FDR) ~ 1.5 is the single FDR<0.05 depleted hit (rare-codon flagged). One zero-cell hydrophobic point sits at log2(OR) ~ -10 at the far-left edge — visible but not significant. All other codons cluster near the origin.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/codon/individual/BWM_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-5 BWM E-site codon volcano: **completely null panel** — no glyph crosses the FDR=0.05 horizontal threshold (all triangles absent; only circles drawn). All 61 codons sit in a tight cluster between log2(OR) ~ -1 and +1 with -log10(FDR) <= 1.0. The smallest p_adj across the panel is 0.114. This is the visual signature of the `larger-bh-family` caveat — aa-level BWM,E:G/R/I signal in the companion file is spread across multiple synonyms here.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/codon/individual/BWM_E_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-5 control A-site codon volcano: the most populated BWM-companion panel. CAA (Gln, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~ -0.86, -log10(FDR) ~ 6.0 sits at the top-y depletion; GGA (Gly, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~ +0.45, -log10(FDR) ~ 5.9 is the matched-y enrichment. AAC (Asn, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~ -0.57, -log10(FDR) ~ 3.5 is the next-highest FDR<0.05 depletion. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~ +0.47, -log10(FDR) ~ 1.8 is FDR<0.05 enriched; CCA (Pro, neutral orange) sits just at the FDR threshold. ~5 codons cross FDR<0.05 total.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/codon/individual/control_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-5 control P-site codon volcano: GGA (Gly, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~ +0.52, -log10(FDR) ~ 8.3 sits as the single tall outlier (the file's minimum p_adj). ATT (Ile, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~ -0.55, -log10(FDR) ~ 1.9 and CAA (Gln, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~ -0.56, -log10(FDR) ~ 1.9 are the next-highest FDR<0.05 hits. GTC (Val, hydrophobic green), CTT (Leu, green), and ATC (Ile, green) all sit clustered near the FDR threshold at log2(OR) ~ -0.4 to -0.3 (all three depleted) with marginal triangles. ~6 codons cross FDR<0.05 total.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/codon/individual/control_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-5 control E-site codon volcano: GCC (Ala, hydrophobic green), CTC (Leu, hydrophobic green), and CCA (Pro, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~ +0.47 to +0.61 sit at the top-y enrichments, all three at -log10(FDR) ~ 3.0 (the 3-way `p_adj`-tie at 0.00111). ACC (Thr, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~ +0.48 and GAG (Glu, acidic red) at log2(OR) ~ -0.29 sit clustered just below at -log10(FDR) ~ 2.2; GAG is the only FDR<0.05 depleted hit. GTC (Val, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~ +0.32 and ATC (Ile, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~ +0.29 sit at the FDR threshold. Range is tight (+/-1 log2(OR)).](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/codon/individual/control_E_volcano.png)
